2 Data Preparation¶

In [ ]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegressionCV
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
import seaborn as sns
from scipy import stats
from IPython import display

np.random.seed(72023)
pd.set_option('display.max_columns', None)
In [ ]:
csv_churn = pd.read_csv('churn_clean.csv')

csv_churn

10000 rows × 50 columns

In [ ]:
df = csv_churn.copy().loc[:, ~csv_churn.columns.isin(['Customer_id', 'Interaction', 'UID',
                                     'County', 'Zip', 'Lat', 'Lng', 'Population',
                                     'TimeZone', 'Job', 'Item1', 'Item2',
                                     'Item3', 'Item4', 'Item5', 'Item6',
                                     'Item7', 'Item8', 'CaseOrder', 'City'])]

df.info()

In [ ]:
df_quant = df.select_dtypes(include=('float64', 'int64'))
df_cat = df[['State', 'Area', 'Marital', 'Gender', 'Contract',
                'InternetService', 'PaymentMethod']]
df_y_n = df.loc[:, ~df.columns.isin(df_quant + df_cat)]
scaler = StandardScaler()
scaled_quant = pd.DataFrame(scaler.fit_transform(df_quant), 
                            columns=df_quant.columns)
ohe = OneHotEncoder(sparse_output=False)
encoded_cat = pd.DataFrame(ohe.fit_transform(df_cat), 
                           columns=ohe.get_feature_names_out(df_cat.columns))
df_processed = pd.concat([scaled_quant, encoded_cat], axis=1)
for col in df_y_n:
    df_processed[col] = df_y_n[col].map({'Yes':1, 'No':0})

df_processed

10000 rows × 96 columns

In [ ]:
X = df_processed.loc[:, df_processed.columns !='Churn']
y = df_processed['Churn']

3 Factors Correlated with High Churn Rates¶

3.1 Identification

In [ ]:
logR_lasso = LogisticRegressionCV(penalty='l1',
                                solver='liblinear')
logR_lasso.fit(X, y)
logR_lasso_coef = pd.DataFrame(logR_lasso.coef_, columns=X.columns).iloc[:, np.where(logR_lasso.coef_ != 0)[1]]
In [ ]:
rf = RandomForestClassifier()
rf.fit(X, y)
rf_feat_imp = pd.DataFrame(rf.feature_importances_.reshape(1,-1), columns=X.columns)
In [ ]:
gb = GradientBoostingClassifier()
gb.fit(X, y)
gb_feat_imp = pd.DataFrame(gb.feature_importances_.reshape(1,-1), columns=X.columns)
In [ ]:
logR_lasso_coef_abs_sorted = logR_lasso_coef.iloc[:, logR_lasso_coef.iloc[0].abs().argsort()[::-1]]
rf_feat_imp_sorted = rf_feat_imp.iloc[:, rf_feat_imp.iloc[0].argsort()[::-1]]
gb_feat_imp_sorted = gb_feat_imp.iloc[:, gb_feat_imp.iloc[0].argsort()[::-1]]

plt.plot(logR_lasso_coef_abs_sorted.iloc[:, 0:15].abs().values.flatten(),
         color='#004c6d', linewidth=1,
         marker='o', markersize=3)
plt.title("Logistic Lasso Regression - Sorted Coefficients")
plt.xlabel("Index")
plt.xticks(range(15))
plt.ylabel("Coefficient")
plt.show()

plt.plot(rf_feat_imp_sorted.iloc[:, 0:15].values.flatten(),
         color='#004c6d', linewidth=1,
         marker='o', markersize=3)
plt.title("Random Forest - Sorted Feature Importances")
plt.xlabel("Index")
plt.xticks(range(15))
plt.ylabel("Feature Importance")
plt.show()

plt.plot(gb_feat_imp_sorted.iloc[:, 0:15].values.flatten(),
         color='#004c6d', linewidth=1,
         marker='o', markersize=3)
plt.title("Gradient Boosting - Sorted Feature Importances")
plt.xlabel("Index")
plt.xticks(range(15))
plt.ylabel("Feature Importance")
plt.show()
In [ ]:
corr_variables = pd.Series(list(logR_lasso_coef_abs_sorted.iloc[:, 0:5].columns) + 
                 list(rf_feat_imp_sorted.iloc[:, 0:3].columns) +
                 list(gb_feat_imp_sorted.iloc[:, 0:3].columns))
corr_variables = list(corr_variables[corr_variables.duplicated()].unique())

corr_variables
Out[ ]:
['Tenure', 'MonthlyCharge']

3.2 Correlation Visualization

In [ ]:
my_palette = sns.color_palette(['#665191', '#ff7c43'])
sns.scatterplot(data=df,
                x='Tenure', y='MonthlyCharge',
                hue='Churn', alpha = 0.8, 
                palette=my_palette)
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.18, 1), 
           loc='upper right', 
           reverse=True, 
           title="Churn",
           shadow=True)
plt.xlabel("Tenure (Months)")
plt.ylabel("Monthly Charge (US$)")
plt.xticks(range(0, 72, 6))
plt.title("Monthly Charge, Tenure, & Churn Correlation")
plt.show()

3.3 Defining “At Risk” Customers

In [ ]:
X_subset = df.loc[:, df.columns.isin(['MonthlyCharge', 'Tenure'])]
In [ ]:
logR = LogisticRegressionCV()
logR.fit(X_subset, y)
df['atRisk_prob'] = logR.predict_proba(X_subset)[:,1]
df['atRisk'] = logR.predict(X_subset)
df['atRisk'] = df['atRisk'].map({1:'Yes', 0:'No'})

print(df.info())
df

In [ ]:
sns.scatterplot(data=df,
                x='Tenure', y='MonthlyCharge',
                hue='atRisk_prob', alpha = 0.8,
                palette='flare_r')
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.206, 1), 
           loc='upper right', 
           reverse=True,
           title="Probability",
           shadow=True)
plt.xlabel("Tenure (Months)")
plt.ylabel("Monthly Charge (US$)")
plt.xticks(range(0, 72, 6))
plt.title(f'Logistic Regression "At Risk" Probabilities')
plt.show()
In [ ]:
my_palette_r = sns.color_palette(['#ff7c43', '#665191'])
sns.scatterplot(data=df,
                x='Tenure', y='MonthlyCharge',
                hue='atRisk', alpha = 0.8,
                palette=my_palette_r)
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.18, 1), 
           loc='upper right',
           title=f'"At Risk"',
           shadow=True)
plt.xlabel("Tenure (Months)")
plt.ylabel("Monthly Charge (US$)")
plt.xticks(range(0, 72, 6))
plt.title("Logistic Regression Classification")
plt.show()
In [ ]:
df_atRisk = df.copy().loc[df['atRisk']=='Yes', :]
df_atRisk_quant = df_atRisk.select_dtypes(include=('float64', 'int64'))
df_atRisk_quant = df_atRisk_quant.loc[:, ~df_atRisk_quant.columns.isin(['MonthlyCharge', 'Tenure', 'atRisk_prob'])]
df_atRisk_cat = df_atRisk.select_dtypes(include='object')
df_atRisk_cat = df_atRisk_cat.loc[:, ~df_atRisk_cat.columns.isin(['Churn', 'atRisk'])]

print(df_atRisk.info())

4 Variables Correlated with Retention in “At Risk” Subset¶

In [ ]:
p_values_quant = []
for col in df_atRisk_quant.columns:
    yes = df_atRisk.loc[df_atRisk['Churn']=='Yes', col]
    no = df_atRisk.loc[df_atRisk['Churn']=='No', col]
    p_value = stats.mannwhitneyu(yes, no).pvalue
    p_values_quant.append(p_value)
df_quant_p = pd.DataFrame([p_values_quant], columns=df_atRisk_quant.columns)
In [ ]:
p_values_cat = []
for col in df_atRisk_cat.columns:
    contingency_table = pd.crosstab(df_atRisk['Churn'], df_atRisk[col])
    p_value = stats.chi2_contingency(contingency_table)[1]
    p_values_cat.append(p_value)
df_cat_p = pd.DataFrame([p_values_cat], columns=df_atRisk_cat.columns)
In [ ]:
sig_p = pd.concat([df_quant_p.loc[:, df_quant_p.iloc[0] <= 0.05],
                                        df_cat_p.loc[:, df_cat_p.iloc[0] <= 0.05]])
sig_var = sig_p.columns.tolist()

sig_var

                                        df_cat_p.loc[:, df_cat_p.iloc[0] <= 0.05/30]])
